title: Conjecture 1 (Botler–Colucci–Kohayakawa)
statement_text: Let $k\geq 2$ be an integer. There is a constant $C$ such that $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq k+C$ for every graph $G$.
context_text: Botler, Colucci, and Kohayakawa proved $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq 198k-101$ and conjectured the multiplicative constant can be reduced to 1. The bound $k$ is tight up to the additive constant $C$ since $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(K_{1,k})=k$, and $C\geq 2$ since $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(K_{1,k,k})=k+2$. Nweit and Yang later improved the multiplicative constant to 177.
paper_contribution: Proves $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq 9k+f(k)$ for a function $f\in o(k)$ (and more precisely $\leq 7k+f(k)$ when $k$ is odd), reducing the best-known multiplicative constant from 177 to 9.
paper_title: On Modular Edge Colourings of Graphs

title: Conjecture 4 (Alon–Friedland–Kalai)
statement_text: Let $k$ be a positive integer. If $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices that does not contain a non-empty $k$-divisible subgraph, then $e(G)\leq(k-1)n$.
context_text: Alon, Friedland, and Kalai conjectured in 1984 that their theorem on $k$-regular subgraphs generalises to all $k$. Botler, Colucci, and Kohayakawa proved a weaker version replacing the coefficient $(k-1)$ by $(24k-12)$; the present paper sharpens this coefficient further.
paper_contribution: Proves a strengthened version of the previously known weaker bound, improving the edge-count coefficient beyond the $(24k-12)n$ barrier established by Botler–Colucci–Kohayakawa.
paper_title: On Modular Edge Colourings of Graphs

title: Conjecture 13
statement_text: Let $k$ be a positive integer. For every $0_{k}$-graph $G$ we have $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq k+o(k)$.
context_text: A $0_k$-graph is a graph whose relevant subgraph structure satisfies a $0\pmod{k}$ degree condition. If Conjecture 13 holds, the proof strategy of Theorem 2 can be adapted so that a maximal $0_k$-subgraph $H$ of any graph $G$ is coloured with $\leq k+o(k)$ colours and the remainder (which would then be $(2q(k)-2)$-degenerate) is coloured via Lemma 9, yielding an overall bound approaching $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq k+o(k)$ for all graphs.
paper_title: On Modular Edge Colourings of Graphs

title: Conjecture 14
statement_text: Let $k$ be a positive integer. For every $0_{q(k)}$-graph $G$ we have $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq 3k+o(k)$.
context_text: Here $q(k)$ denotes the smallest prime $\geq k$. If Conjecture 14 holds, one can identify a maximal $0_{q(k)}$-subgraph $H$ and colour it with $\leq 3k+o(k)$ colours; the remainder $G'$ contains no non-empty $q(k)$-divisible subgraph and is thus $(2q(k)-2)$-degenerate, colourable with $\leq 5k+o(k)$ colours via Lemma 9, giving the overall bound $\chi^{\prime}_{k}(G)\leq 8k+o(k)$.
paper_title: On Modular Edge Colourings of Graphs
